candidate_summary: K-route multi-criteria conjunctive eligibility-gate (intent + content + format + safety classifiers) with per-route sub-style top-K-head matching (Khalkh/Buriad/Uriankhai-style) and compound-input mixture-of-route convex output; supervised classifier-trained gates inspired by Mongolian Naadam 'three games of men' eligibility rules.

results.name: One Head Many Models Cross-Attention Routing Cost-Aware (2509.09782)
results.description: Single-head cross-attention routing for cost-aware LLM selection — jointly models query + model embeddings.
results.rationale: Cross-attention routing for model selection shares the routing-via-attention paradigm with NAADAM's K-route eligibility gate; the cost-aware selection plays a similar functional role to a multi-criteria gate, but it is single-head and does not implement explicit conjunctive eligibility classifiers or sub-style top-K-head matching.

results.name: Dynamic Model Routing Cascading Survey (2603.04445)
results.description: Dynamic routing + cascading survey; multi-criteria routing patterns.
keyword_matches: K-route multi-criteria eligibility-gate input routing (multi-criteria routing)
supervised intent-classifier gate learning (classifier-based routing patterns)
results.rationale: Survey of dynamic + multi-criteria routing/cascading covers the broad K-route eligibility-gate paradigm; functional overlap because cascade-style classifiers act as conjunctive gates.

results.name: vLLM Semantic Router Flash Attention Compression (2603.12646)
results.description: 98x faster routing without dedicated GPU; safety classification + intent routing.
keyword_matches: K-route multi-criteria eligibility-gate input routing (intent routing + safety classification)
gender age sub-style multi-criteria gate (multi-criteria via safety+intent)
results.rationale: vLLM semantic router combines safety classification with intent routing — directly mirrors NAADAM's conjunctive multi-criteria classifier gate (intent + safety). Strong functional twin minus the per-route sub-style top-K-head and compound mixture aspects.

results.name: Hierarchical Routers Top-k Retrieval Sparse Attention
results.description: Hierarchical routers for efficient top-k retrieval in sparse attention; dynamic top-k tokens per-sequence.
keyword_matches: per-route sub-style top-K-head matching (hierarchical top-k routing)
K-route multi-criteria eligibility-gate input routing (hierarchical routers)
results.rationale: Hierarchical top-k routing in sparse attention closely parallels per-route top-K-head matching; hierarchy gives the K-route structure. Lacks the explicit multi-criteria classifier-gate and compound mixture-of-route convex output.

results.name: Gated Attention NeurIPS 2025 Oral
results.description: Head-specific sigmoid gate; non-linearity + sparsity + attention-sink-free.
results.rationale: Head-specific sigmoid gate is structurally similar to per-route head-level gating, sitting on the threshold of functional twin; differs by not being multi-criteria conjunctive nor route-based mixture, only a single sigmoid per head.

results.name: Union of Experts Hierarchical Routing UoE (2503.02495)
results.description: UoE hierarchical routing strategy dynamically routes input subspaces to activated experts via sparse gating.
keyword_matches: K-route multi-criteria eligibility-gate input routing (hierarchical routing + sparse gating)
per-route sub-style top-K-head matching (subspace routing to experts)
results.rationale: Union-of-Experts hierarchical sparse gating over input subspaces is functionally a near-twin of K-route gated routing with mixture output; missing only the conjunctive multi-criteria classifier framing.

results.name: MoA Mixture of Attention Heads Per-Token EMNLP 2022
results.description: MoA Router selects k attention heads per token; weighted sum output across selected heads.
keyword_matches: per-route sub-style top-K-head matching (selects k heads per token)
compound-input mixture-of-route output (weighted sum across heads)
K-route multi-criteria eligibility-gate input routing (MoA router)
results.rationale: MoA's per-token routing to top-K heads with weighted-sum output is essentially the same mechanism as NAADAM's per-route top-K-head matching + compound mixture-of-route output. Strongest functional twin.

results.name: Cost-Aware LLM Routing NadirClaw 2026
results.description: Prompt-classification + cost-aware routing + multi-model switching; multi-criteria gate.
keyword_matches: K-route multi-criteria eligibility-gate input routing (multi-criteria gate, multi-model routing)
gender age sub-style multi-criteria gate (multi-criteria gate)
results.rationale: NadirClaw's prompt-classification + multi-criteria cost-aware routing maps closely to NAADAM's classifier-trained K-route eligibility-gate routing. Differs by lacking per-route sub-style top-K-head matching and convex mixture output.

decision_rationale: Independent verification finds all 8 retrieved results clear prior-art twins or near-twins of NAADAM-MULTI-GATE's claimed mechanism. MoA (2022) already implements per-token top-K head selection with weighted-sum mixture — the central novelty claim. UoE provides hierarchical multi-route sparse gating with mixture output, vLLM Semantic Router provides multi-criteria (intent+safety) conjunctive classifier gating, NadirClaw provides supervised classifier-trained multi-criteria routing, Gated Attention provides per-head gating, and Hierarchical Routers provide per-route top-K matching. Together these collectively cover (a) K-route multi-criteria classifier gate, (b) per-route top-K-head matching, and (c) compound mixture-of-route output. Candidate is a recombination of mainstream gated-routing + MoA components; novelty is absent. Result: FAIL.
